reasoning.A:
DECISION: Verify: Q2  
REASONING: Volunteering in Round 1 would require trusting three completely unverified answers (Q2–Q4), which is unnecessary risk—especially given I’ve already died once in a previous game for volunteering off weakly checked capsules. Passing or verifying gives me the same risk of random elimination if nobody volunteers, but verifying Q2 for 1 coin improves group knowledge and builds consensus on at least one other part of the password. Based on past games, this also complements others’ usual verification patterns, increasing the chance that when someone does volunteer, they’ll have a safer set of capsules to use.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Pass  
REASONING: I’m confident in my own Q2 answer, so verifying Q2 would waste a coin. Verifying any other question this round still leaves me with essentially the same random-death risk if nobody volunteers, but costs 1 coin and only helps in later rounds. In all recent games, A has verified Q2 and C has volunteered in Round 1, leading to immediate escape, so the probability that everyone passes is low. Passing preserves my full 4 coins without materially changing my survival odds this round.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own puzzle (7 × 11) is trivial, so I know Q3-C is correct. Historically, with these simple arithmetic puzzles we’ve only seen one wrong answer (D’s Q4 in an early game), and in every recent game the “one capsule per question” set has been correct when I volunteered. That makes the chance that at least one of A, B, or D is wrong plausibly well below the 25% death risk from random elimination if nobody volunteers.  

If I instead verify this round, I still face that same 25% random-death risk (since I can’t both verify and volunteer in the same round), and I’d lose a coin for information that only helps in later rounds—if I survive. Given the past pattern that A and B tend to verify rather than volunteer and D often passes, the bigger risk is that no one volunteers.  

So volunteering now with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D maximizes my survival probability this round while preserving all 4 of my coins if the password is correct.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
🔍 Verifications completed:
  ✅ A agrees with Q2-B → now Q2-B+A (-1 coin: 4→3)

✅ C entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A, Q2-B+A, Q3-C, Q4-D
🎉 Everyone escapes! 🎉
